SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Arterial Spray from the Right Arm
SCENARIOS.hint: Massive Bleed on Extremity ... TQ Immediately

SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Casualty Unconscious ... Snoring , No Bleeding
SCENARIOS.hint: Snoring + Unconscious ... NPA and AVPU Checks

SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Sucking Chest Wound from Shrapnel
SCENARIOS.hint: Open Chest Wound ... Occlusive Chest Seal

SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Casualty is Shivering in the Rain after a Fall
SCENARIOS.hint: Prevent Hypothermia ...

SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Simple Fracture on Forearm ... Stable
SCENARIOS.hint: Standard Extremity Fracture ... Sam Splint.

SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Minor GSW to Calf ... Bleeding is Slow and Dark
SCENARIOS.hint: Non-Arterial Bleed ... Direct Pressure + Antibiotics

SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Deep Laceration to the Shoulder ... No TQ Possible
SCENARIOS.hint: Junctional Bleed ( Shoulder ) ... Wound Packing

SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Face Burned by Flash Bang ... Alert and Talking
SCENARIOS.hint: Flash Burns with NO Airway Distress ... Wet Dressings

SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Casualty has Broken Ankle , Moderate Pain ... Alert
SCENARIOS.hint: Stable Pain Management ... Fentanyl

SCENARIOS.sitrep: EASY : Small GSW to the Abdomen ... Conscious
SCENARIOS.hint: Abdominal Wounds ... Abdominal Dressing + Ertapenem

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : GSW to Groin with Heavy bleeding ... Casualty is Drowsy
SCENARIOS.hint: Junctional Bleed + Altered Mental Status ... Packing , IV , Ketamine

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : Blast victim Missing Left Foot ... Breathing Fast and Shallow
SCENARIOS.hint: TQ for Amputation . Chest Seal for Respiratory Rate ( Frag ) , Ketamine for Shock

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : Gurgling Respirations , Gunshot to the Jaw ... Conscious
SCENARIOS.hint: Jaw thrust // leaning Forward for Airway . Dry dressing for Facial Wounds

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : Suspected Pelvic Fracture ... Weak Radial Pulse
SCENARIOS.hint: Pelvic Binder + Blanket for Shock Prevention

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : 30% Body Burns ... Shivering ... Extreme Pain
SCENARIOS.hint: Large Burns ... Dry Dressings . Prevent Heat Loss . Ketamine for Pain

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : Penetrating Chest Wound ... Trachea is Shifted to the Side
SCENARIOS.hint: Tracheal Deviation = Tension Pneumothorax ... Perform Needle D.

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : Fall with Head Injury ... Pupillary Response Unequal
SCENARIOS.hint: Head Injury = Monitor AVPU closely . Manual Airway Only

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : GSW to the Arm ... TQ Applied but Bleeding Continues
SCENARIOS.hint: Bleeding through First TQ ... Apply a Second > 'Side-by-Side' TQ

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : Shrapnel to the Thigh ... Massive Bleed and Casualty is Screaming
SCENARIOS.hint: TQ First ... Fentanyl is SAFE if they are Screaming ( stable vitals )

SCENARIOS.sitrep: MEDIUM : Chemical Burn to Eyes and Skin ... Respiratory Distress
SCENARIOS.hint: NPA for Airway , Occlusive for Chest , Wet Dressings for Chemical Burns

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : Double Amputee ( Legs ) ... No Radial Pulse and Gasping for Air
SCENARIOS.hint: Aggressive MARCH : TQs , NPA, Needle D ( for Gasping ) , Fluids for Shock

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : GSW to Chest and Groin ... Chest Seal Applied but Failing
SCENARIOS.hint: Pack the Groin . Needle D for the FAILING Chest Seal . Ketamine for Pain

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : Near-Drowning , Blast Victim ... Cold to Touch , Gurgling
SCENARIOS.hint: Suction the Airway . HPMK for Hypothermia . Fluids for Circulation

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : Multiple Shrapnel Wounds ... Abdomen Evisceration , 40% Burns
SCENARIOS.hint: Evisceration = Abdominal Dressing . Burns = Blanket + Dry Dressing

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : Blunt Force to Chest , Paradoxical Breathing ( Flail Chest ) ... Unconscious.
SCENARIOS.hint: Flail Chest NEEDS an Occlusive // Bulky Bressing . NPA/AVPU for Unconsciousness

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : GSW to the Buttock ... Deep Internal Bleeding , Casualty is Pale
SCENARIOS.hint: Pack the Wound ( Junctional ) . IV/HPMK for Impending Shock

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : Crush Injury ( Leg trapped 2 hours ) ... Dark Urine Noted
SCENARIOS.hint: TQ BEFORE Release . Fluids for Crush Syndrome . Ketamine for Pain

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : Snake Bite Lower Limb ... Swelling Rapidly
SCENARIOS.hint: Pressure Immobilization . Fluids and Splinting . Fentanyl for Pain

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : Head Trauma + Extremity Bleed ... BP 80/40 , Pupil Dilated
SCENARIOS.hint: Aggressive Fluids , Resuscitation iot Maintain MAP for Head Injury

SCENARIOS.sitrep: HARD : IED Blast ... Blinded , Shrapnel in Neck , High Anxiety
SCENARIOS.hint: Pack Neck Wounds . Ketamine ( LOW Dose ) for 'Chemical Restraint' ... Anxiety

SCENARIOS.sitrep: NINJA : Mass Cas ... Victim 1 : Amputee, NO Pulse . Victim 2 : Screaming , GSW Leg
SCENARIOS.hint: Focus on the Silent Amputee First ... TQ , NPA , Fluids Immediately

SCENARIOS.sitrep: NINJA : GSW Torso , Chest Seal in Place . Cyanosis . No Radial Pulse . Cold // Clammy
SCENARIOS.hint: Total System Failure ... Needle D ( Respiration ) + IO Fluids ( Circulation ) = Life-Saving

SCENARIOS.sitrep: NINJA : 60% Burns . Blast Lung . Pelvic Crush . Unconscious
SCENARIOS.hint: TFC Challenge : NPA , Occlusive , Binder , HPMK , Ketamine
